pr-evidence/directory-documentation-gate.gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 found two low-severity issues: the directory documentation gate scans untracked directories, and template README wording could imply the packaged template tree need not stay importable. The untracked scan is intentional for local PR slices and is now documented in the helper docstring; the README now describes templates as importable resources rather than executable runtime modules.
scope: template resource listing and extraction tests
verification: find src tests -name '*.py' -print0 | xargs -0 python -m py_compile
what_changed: Added packaged template-root READMEs for src/document_kv_cache/templates and src/document_kv_cache/templates/databricks.
Updated template resource tests so the new READMEs are listed, packaged, and extractable.
Added a governance assertion that packaged template-root READMEs name their immediate subfolders.
Documented why the directory documentation helper scans tracked and untracked directories during local PR work.
pr-evidence/directory-documentation-gate.why: Keep every shipped folder self-documenting and make packaged Databricks template resources understandable after wheel extraction, without relying on source checkout context.
